page.content: In a study comparing Sputum-Scarce with Sputum-Producing populations, 187 and 2,341 participants were analyzed, revealing a mean age of 36.3 (9.6) years for Sputum-Scarce and 37.9 (10.5) years for Sputum-Producing with a p-value of 0.031. The sex distribution was 92:95 (49%) for males in Sputum-Scarce and 1,136:1,205 (49%) in Sputum-Producing, with a p-value of 0.86. The mean CD4+ count showed values of 178 (241) cells/mm3 for Sputum-Scarce and 150 (176) cells/mm3 for Sputum-Producing, yielding a p-value of 0.15. Mean Karnofsky scores were 50.6 (12.4) for Sputum-Scarce and 53.4 (16.1) for Sputum-Producing, with a significant p-value of 0.005. Mean weights were 47.8 (9.1) kg and 53.4 (12.0) kg, respectively, with a p-value of <0.001. Mean BMI values were 17.2 (3.3) for Sputum-Scarce and 19.5 (4.4) for Sputum-Producing, also with a p-value of <0.001. In another analysis of products, Duralay (PMMA) showed values of 10.03 ± 1.76 at A 23 °C, 9.86 ± 2.40 at B 37 °C, and 7.42 ± 3.42 at C 60 °C, categorized as NS. Trim II (PEMA) had results of 14.20 ± 3.06 at A 23 °C, 13.77 ± 4.68 at B 37 °C, and 9.00 ± 4.40 at C 60 °C, with findings A, B > C. Luxatemp (BAC) showed 3.84 ± 3.17 at A 23 °C, 6.33 ± 1.72 at B 37 °C, and 9.52 ± 1.88 at C 60 °C, classified as A < C. Protemp 4 (BAC) had values of 3.17 ± 0.60 at A 23 °C, 2.16 ± 0.95 at B 37 °C, and 5.77 ± 2.10 at C 60 °C, marked as NS. The Bonferroni results indicated 4 < 3 < 1 < 2 at A 23 °C, 3, 4 < 2, 4 < 1 at B 37 °C, and 4 < 3 at C 60 °C.
